extraction.one_line_summary: GENIEZ Alexandre won mountaintop sprint from reformed breakaway, holding off VAN BAAL Dylan's late charge in difficult conditions
tactical_patterns: HARADA Jesus controlled tempo at breakaway front to protect 5:45 GC deficit, nearest rider to overall contention.
Breakaway reformed on climb with quartet establishing gap before VAN BAAL bridged, suggesting initial selection too weak.
YATES Simon personally chased when no teammates stepped up, unusual GC leader workload at 10:17 gap.

rider_signals.GENIEZ Alexandre: positive: Won mountaintop sprint despite weaker climbing pedigree than VAN BAAL, suggesting good form in harsh conditions
rider_signals.VAN BAAL Dylan: positive: Bridged solo to leading quartet on climb then contested sprint, versatility beyond sprinter profile
rider_signals.HARADA Jesus: neutral: Protected GC position by controlling breakaway tempo, 5:45 down made him key collaborator
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: Forced to chase personally when peloton sat passive at 10+ minutes, team unable to control
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: Dropped early confirming stage too hard for pure sprinters despite initial speculation
